title: Museum Technicians and Conservators
description: Restore, maintain, or prepare objects in museum collections for storage, research, or exhibit. May work with specimens such as fossils, skeletal parts, or botanicals; or artifacts, textiles, or art. May identify and record objects or install and arrange them in exhibits. Includes book or document conservators.

tasks.text: Install, arrange, assemble, and prepare artifacts for exhibition, ensuring the artifacts' safety, reporting their status and condition, and identifying and correcting any problems with the set up.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair, restore, and reassemble artifacts, designing and fabricating missing or broken parts, to restore them to their original appearance and prevent deterioration.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Clean objects, such as paper, textiles, wood, metal, glass, rock, pottery, and furniture, using cleansers, solvents, soap solutions, and polishes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Photograph objects for documentation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine whether objects need repair and choose the safest and most effective method of repair.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare artifacts for storage and shipping.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Enter information about museum collections into computer databases.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Recommend preservation procedures, such as control of temperature and humidity, to curatorial and building staff.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Notify superior when restoration of artifacts requires outside experts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise and work with volunteers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform on-site field work which may involve interviewing people, inspecting and identifying artifacts, note-taking, viewing sites and collections, and repainting exhibition spaces.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Lead tours and teach educational courses to students and the general public.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Classify and assign registration numbers to artifacts and supervise inventory control.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Study object documentation or conduct standard chemical and physical tests to ascertain the object's age, composition, original appearance, need for treatment or restoration, and appropriate preservation method.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare reports on the operation of conservation laboratories, documenting the condition of artifacts, treatment options, and the methods of preservation and repair used.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Specialize in particular materials or types of object, such as documents and books, paintings, decorative arts, textiles, metals, or architectural materials.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform tests and examinations to establish storage and conservation requirements, policies, and procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Direct and supervise curatorial, technical, and student staff in the handling, mounting, care, and storage of art objects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Coordinate exhibit installations, assisting with design, constructing displays, dioramas, display cases, and models, and ensuring the availability of necessary materials.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Preserve or direct preservation of objects, using plaster, resin, sealants, hardeners, and shellac.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan and conduct research to develop and improve methods of restoring and preserving specimens.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Deliver artwork on courier trips.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Build, repair, and install wooden steps, scaffolds, and walkways to gain access to or permit improved view of exhibited equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Estimate cost of restoration work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
